justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Accrual rate (BR-1) and carryover cap (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false. Negative balance (BR-2), rejected leave balance integrity (BR-5), and contract boundary (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: true. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (partial month accrual, concurrent requests, retroactive window anchor, calendar vs working days). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 68 is well-calibrated given the ambiguities identified.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified with precise invariant classifications and strong justifications for each
Clear distinction between structural domain invariants (balance integrity, contract boundary) and configurable HR policies (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice period)
12 acceptance criteria covering accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical leave exception, rejection balance integrity, contract boundary, and carryover — far exceeding the 4 minimum
Rich ambiguity surfacing: partial month accrual, concurrent request balance holds, retroactive window anchor, calendar vs working days
Excellent edge cases including contract amendment after approval, year-end carryover with pending requests, and contract ending on Dec 31
Business impact analysis correctly identifies leave as a financial liability and covers all relevant stakeholder groups
Confidence level of 68 is appropriately calibrated — acknowledges clear rules while flagging genuine operational gaps
